features.explanation: Fires on tokens that are highly predictable second elements of common bigrams or fixed expressions (e.g., preposition-object, adjective-noun, discourse-adverb + comma).
features.token_pattern: Function words, punctuation, numbers, or morpheme endings that complete very common two-token sequences
features.context_pattern: The token appears as the strongly expected continuation of the immediately preceding token in a frequent collocation or fixed phrase
features.corpus_consistency: Yes, it activates on the same type of predictable bigram continuations in both flores and xsafety texts

features.explanation: This feature fires on tokens that begin a new word (i.e., tokens with a leading space), especially common words, numbers, or punctuation marks.
features.token_pattern: tokens that include a leading space and represent the first subtoken of a new word, including common words, numbers, and punctuation
features.context_pattern: appears broadly across many contexts without semantic specificity, simply marking the start of a new word in running text
features.corpus_consistency: yes, it consistently fires on word-initial tokens across both flores and xsafety corpora

features.explanation: This feature fires on short standalone tokens at word boundaries, including single digits, punctuation marks, short function words, and common subword fragments.
features.token_pattern: single-character digits, punctuation (e.g., ',', ':'), short words like 'all', 'an', 'at', and common subword pieces such as 'MS' or 'ality'
features.context_pattern: appears in ordinary prose across many topics, with activations concentrated on brief tokens that form complete words or subword fragments within larger words or numbers
features.corpus_consistency: yes, the same pattern of short boundary tokens appears in both flores and xsafety examples

features.explanation: This feature fires on tokens that begin a new word (i.e., tokens with a leading space in the tokenizer), regardless of their semantic content.

features.context_pattern: Appears in arbitrary sentences across topics, with no consistent semantic theme beyond the token being at a word boundary

features.explanation: Activates on tokens that follow multiple consecutive spaces, capturing formatting/alignment artifacts in structured text.

features.context_pattern: formatted or structured text with irregular spacing such as verse numbers, lists, percentages, parentheticals, or dialogue formatting
features.corpus_consistency: yes, it fires on the same spacing/alignment artifact in both flores and xsafety examples

features.safety_relevance: Many activations occur in harmful-intent or safety-related advice-seeking questions, but the feature itself reflects generic question syntax rather than harmful content.
features.corpus_consistency: Yes, it appears consistently tied to interrogative question forms across contexts.
